element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary ranged weapon unlocked at game start
Directional aiming mechanic for aerial threats
Vertical traversal opens new navigation paths
Progressive power scaling through pickups, max 5 levels
Advanced projectile technique with increased range
Complex aerial attack for enemies below
Alternative projectile mode with diagonal upward travel
Specific enemy targets shown before stage; energy collection gates progression
10-second window to enter portal after opening; failure = life loss and restart
Enables damage during flip jumps; explicitly offered by antagonist as progression aid
Narrative tension device; antagonist provides cryptic warnings and hints
Foreshadowing of parasitic implant twist ending

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating target enemy and collecting energy
typical_tension_triggers: Timed 10-second window to enter dimensional door after opening (instant life loss on failure)
Multiple targets requiring destruction before energy accumulation
Power level reduction upon taking damage
Mandatory specific target designation per stage
Parasitic implant pain intensification as story progresses
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Stage-gated progression with hard time pressure on final portal entry; combat-driven energy accumulation creates variable pacing within stages

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Power-up capsule containers hidden throughout stages; destructible item containers contain optional upgrades that increase maximum power level ceiling
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden power-up capsules scattered in item containers; flip shield capsule specifically hidden on designated planet (Planet 3/Sanddune)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational mechanics and single-target objectives establish baseline; energy collection and portal timing introduce pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Multiple target requirements and increased enemy density; power-up acquisition becomes critical for range extension
difficulty_curve.late_game: Parasitic implant condition worsens narratively; final confrontation reveals protagonist is Troy's creation, escalating stakes
notable_spikes: Planet 1 initial 10-second portal deadline introduces core pressure mechanic
Multi-target stages requiring significant energy accumulation before portal opens
Planet 3+ enemy density increases as antagonist offers progression aids (flip shield)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage displays designated enemy target(s) before beginning. Player must destroy specific targets to accumulate sufficient energy, which triggers dimensional door opening. Hard constraint on progression.
Once dimensional door opens after target destruction, player has exactly 10 seconds to enter or loses a life and must restart stage. Creates rhythmic urgency spike at stage end.

Projectile power increases through 5 levels via power-up capsule collection; damage reduces power by 1 level; life loss resets to starting level. Creates risk/reward for power-up hunting vs. combat avoidance.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All stages encourage optional capsule destruction

notable_level_patterns.description: Wall climbing, pole mechanics, and ledge traversal are core navigation verbs alongside projectile combat. Enables avoidance routing and combat positioning.

Mysterious antagonist (Troy/Dr. Jose) taunt Ken across planets 2-4, strategically offering progression aids (flip shield on Planet 3) while warning him to turn back. Creates narrative pacing through dialogue checkpoints.

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Street Fighter 2010 regional differences and story text)
wikipedia (Street Fighter 2010: The Final Fight - gameplay mechanics and plot summary)
